Constants.title: factory → const _Factory
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance or static method m. Indicates that m must either be abstract or must return a newly allocated object or null. In addition, every method that either implements or overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: immutable → const Immutable
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class C. Indicates that C and all subtypes of C must be immutable.

Constants.title: kAlwaysCompleteAnimation → const Animation<double>
Constants.description: An animation that is always complete.

Constants.title: kAlwaysDismissedAnimation → const Animation<double>
Constants.description: An animation that is always dismissed.

Constants.title: kDefaultContentInsertionMimeTypes → const List<String>
Constants.description: The default mime types to be used when allowedMimeTypes is not provided.

Constants.title: kDefaultFontSize → const double
Constants.description: The default font size if none is specified.

Constants.title: kDefaultRouteDirectionalTraversalEdgeBehavior → const TraversalEdgeBehavior
Constants.description: The default value of Navigator.routeDirectionalTraversalEdgeBehavior.

Constants.title: kDefaultRouteTraversalEdgeBehavior → const TraversalEdgeBehavior
Constants.description: The default value of Navigator.routeTraversalEdgeBehavior.

Constants.title: kTextHeightNone → const double
Constants.description: A TextStyle.height value that indicates the text span should take the height defined by the font, which may not be exactly the height of TextStyle.fontSize.

Constants.title: mustCallSuper → const _MustCallSuper
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m. Indicates that every invocation of a member that overrides m must also invoke m. In addition, every method that overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: optionalTypeArgs → const _OptionalTypeArgs
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class, mixin, extension, function, method, or typedef declaration C. Indicates that any type arguments declared on C are to be treated as optional.

Constants.title: protected → const _Protected
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member in a class or mixin which is meant to be visible only within the declaring library, and to other instance members of the class or mixin, and their subtypes.

Constants.title: required → const Required
Constants.description: Used to annotate a named parameter p in a method or function f. Indicates that every invocation of f must include an argument corresponding to p, despite the fact that p would otherwise be an optional parameter.

Constants.title: staticIconProvider → const Object
Constants.description: Annotation for classes that only provide static const IconData instances.

Constants.title: visibleForTesting → const _VisibleForTesting
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration that was made public, so that it is more visible than otherwise necessary, to make code testable.

Constants.title: widgetFactory → const _WidgetFactory
Constants.description: Annotation which marks a function as a widget factory for the purpose of widget creation tracking.

Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m. Indicates that every invocation of a member that overrides m must also invoke m. In addition, every method that overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: optionalTypeArgs → const _OptionalTypeArgs
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class, mixin, extension, function, method, or typedef declaration C. Indicates that any type arguments declared on C are to be treated as optional.

Constants.title: protected → const _Protected
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member in a class or mixin which is meant to be visible only within the declaring library, and to other instance members of the class or mixin, and their subtypes.

Constants.title: required → const Required
Constants.description: Used to annotate a named parameter p in a method or function f. Indicates that every invocation of f must include an argument corresponding to p, despite the fact that p would otherwise be an optional parameter.

Constants.title: staticIconProvider → const Object
Constants.description: Annotation for classes that only provide static const IconData instances.

Constants.title: visibleForTesting → const _VisibleForTesting
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration that was made public, so that it is more visible than otherwise necessary, to make code testable.

Constants.title: widgetFactory → const _WidgetFactory
Constants.description: Annotation which marks a function as a widget factory for the purpose of widget creation tracking.

Exceptions / Errors.title: FlutterError
Exceptions / Errors.description: Error class used to report Flutter-specific assertion failures and contract violations.

Exceptions / Errors.title: NetworkImageLoadException
Exceptions / Errors.description: The exception thrown when the HTTP request to load a network image fails.

Exceptions / Errors.title: TickerCanceled
Exceptions / Errors.description: Exception thrown by Ticker objects on the TickerFuture.orCancel future when the ticker is canceled.

Classes.title: AbstractNode
Classes.description: Deprecated. Unused by the framework and will be removed in a future version of Flutter. If needed, inline any required functionality of this class directly in the subclass.

Classes.title: AggregatedTimedBlock
Classes.description: Aggregates multiple TimedBlock objects that share a name.

Classes.title: AggregatedTimings
Classes.description: Provides aggregated results for timings collected by FlutterTimeline.

Classes.title: BindingBase
Classes.description: Base class for mixins that provide singleton services.

Classes.title: BitField<T extends dynamic>
Classes.description: A BitField over an enum (or other class whose values implement "index"). Only the first 62 values of the enum can be used as indices.

Classes.title: ByteData
Classes.description: A fixed-length, random-access sequence of bytes that also provides random and unaligned access to the fixed-width integers and floating point numbers represented by those bytes.

Classes.title: CachingIterable<E>
Classes.description: A lazy caching version of Iterable.

Classes.title: Category
Classes.description: A category with which to annotate a class, for documentation purposes.

Classes.title: ChangeNotifier
Classes.description: A class that can be extended or mixed in that provides a change notification API using VoidCallback for notifications.

Classes.title: DiagnosticableTreeNode
Classes.description: DiagnosticsNode for an instance of DiagnosticableTree.

Classes.title: DiagnosticPropertiesBuilder
Classes.description: Builder to accumulate properties and configuration used to assemble a DiagnosticsNode from a Diagnosticable object.

Classes.title: DiagnosticsBlock
Classes.description: DiagnosticsNode that exists mainly to provide a container for other diagnostics that typically lacks a meaningful value of its own.

Classes.title: DiagnosticsNode
Classes.description: Defines diagnostics data for a value.

Classes.title: DiagnosticsProperty<T>
Classes.description: Property with a value of type T.

Classes.title: DiagnosticsSerializationDelegate

Classes.title: DiagnosticsStackTrace

Classes.title: DocumentationIcon
Classes.description: A class annotation to provide a URL to an image that represents the class.

Classes.title: DoubleProperty
Classes.description: Property describing a double value with an optional unit of measurement.

Classes.title: Endian
Classes.description: Endianness of number representation.

Classes.title: EnumProperty<T extends Enum?>

Classes.title: ErrorDescription
Classes.description: An explanation of the problem and its cause, any information that may help track down the problem, background information, etc.

Classes.title: ErrorHint
Classes.description: An ErrorHint provides specific, non-obvious advice that may be applicable.

Classes.title: ErrorSpacer
Classes.description: An ErrorSpacer creates an empty DiagnosticsNode, that can be used to tune the spacing between other DiagnosticsNode objects.

Classes.title: ErrorSummary
Classes.description: A short (one line) description of the problem that was detected.

Classes.title: Factory<T>
Classes.description: A factory interface that also reports the type of the created objects.

Classes.title: FlagProperty

Classes.description: Measures how long blocks of code take to run.

Classes.title: LicenseEntryWithLineBreaks
Classes.description: Variant of LicenseEntry for licenses that separate paragraphs with blank lines and that hard-wrap text within paragraphs. Lines that begin with one or more space characters are also assumed to introduce new paragraphs, unless they start with the same number of spaces as the previous line, in which case it's assumed they are a continuation of an indented paragraph.

Constants.title: factory → const _Factory
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance or static method m. Indicates that m must either be abstract or must return a newly allocated object or null. In addition, every method that either implements or overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: immutable → const Immutable
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class C. Indicates that C and all subtypes of C must be immutable.

Constants.title: internal → const _Internal
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration which should only be used from within the package in which it is declared, and which should not be exposed from said package's public API.

Constants.title: kDebugMode → const bool
Constants.description: A constant that is true if the application was compiled in debug mode.

Constants.title: kFlutterMemoryAllocationsEnabled → const bool
Constants.description: If true, Flutter objects dispatch the memory allocation events.

Constants.title: kIsWasm → const bool
Constants.description: A constant that is true if the application was compiled to WebAssembly.

Constants.title: kIsWeb → const bool
Constants.description: A constant that is true if the application was compiled to run on the web.

Constants.title: kMaxUnsignedSMI → const int
Constants.description: The largest SMI value.

Constants.title: kNoDefaultValue → const Object
Constants.description: Marker object indicating that a DiagnosticsNode has no default value.

Constants.title: kProfileMode → const bool
Constants.description: A constant that is true if the application was compiled in profile mode.

Constants.title: kReleaseMode → const bool
Constants.description: A constant that is true if the application was compiled in release mode.

Constants.title: mustCallSuper → const _MustCallSuper
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m. Indicates that every invocation of a member that overrides m must also invoke m. In addition, every method that overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: nonVirtual → const _NonVirtual
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m in a class C or mixin M. Indicates that m should not be overridden in any classes that extend or mixin C or M.

Constants.title: optionalTypeArgs → const _OptionalTypeArgs
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class, mixin, extension, function, method, or typedef declaration C. Indicates that any type arguments declared on C are to be treated as optional.

Constants.title: precisionErrorTolerance → const double
Constants.description: The epsilon of tolerable double precision error.

Constants.title: protected → const _Protected
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member in a class or mixin which is meant to be visible only within the declaring library, and to other instance members of the class or mixin, and their subtypes.

Constants.title: required → const Required
Constants.description: Used to annotate a named parameter p in a method or function f. Indicates that every invocation of f must include an argument corresponding to p, despite the fact that p would otherwise be an optional parameter.

Constants.title: visibleForOverriding → const _VisibleForOverriding
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member that was made public so that it could be overridden but that is not intended to be referenced from outside the defining library.

Constants.title: visibleForTesting → const _VisibleForTesting
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration that was made public, so that it is more visible than otherwise necessary, to make code testable.

Constants.title: kBackMouseButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to the back mouse button.

Constants.title: kDefaultMouseScrollToScaleFactor → const double
Constants.description: The default conversion factor when treating mouse scrolling as scaling.

Constants.title: kDefaultTrackpadScrollToScaleFactor → const Offset
Constants.description: The default conversion factor when treating trackpad scrolling as scaling.

Constants.title: kDoubleTapMinTime → const Duration
Constants.description: The minimum time from the end of the first tap to the start of the second tap in a double-tap gesture.

Constants.title: kDoubleTapSlop → const double
Constants.description: Distance between the initial position of the first touch and the start position of a potential second touch for the second touch to be considered the second touch of a double-tap gesture.

Constants.title: kDoubleTapTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: The maximum time from the start of the first tap to the start of the second tap in a double-tap gesture.

Constants.title: kDoubleTapTouchSlop → const double
Constants.description: The maximum distance that the first touch in a double-tap gesture can travel before deciding that it is not part of a double-tap gesture. DoubleTapGestureRecognizer also restricts the second touch to this distance.

Constants.title: kForwardMouseButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to the forward mouse button.

Constants.title: kHoverTapSlop → const double
Constants.description: Maximum distance between the down and up pointers for a tap. (Currently not honored by the TapGestureRecognizer; PrimaryPointerGestureRecognizer, which TapGestureRecognizer inherits from, uses kTouchSlop.)

Constants.title: kHoverTapTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: Maximum length of time between a tap down and a tap up for the gesture to be considered a tap. (Currently not honored by the TapGestureRecognizer.)

Constants.title: kJumpTapTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: The maximum time from the start of the first tap to the start of the second tap in a jump-tap gesture.

Constants.title: kLongPressTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: The time before a long press gesture attempts to win.

Constants.title: kMaxFlingVelocity → const double
Constants.description: Drag gesture fling velocities are clipped to this value.

Constants.title: kMiddleMouseButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to the middle mouse button.

Constants.title: kMinFlingVelocity → const double
Constants.description: The minimum velocity for a touch to consider that touch to trigger a fling gesture.

Constants.title: kPagingTouchSlop → const double
Constants.description: The distance a touch has to travel for the framework to be confident that the gesture is a paging gesture. (Currently not used, because paging uses a regular drag gesture, which uses kTouchSlop.)

Constants.title: kPanSlop → const double
Constants.description: The distance a touch has to travel for the framework to be confident that the gesture is a panning gesture.

Constants.title: kPrecisePointerHitSlop → const double
Constants.description: Like kTouchSlop, but for more precise pointers like mice and trackpads.

Constants.title: kPrecisePointerPanSlop → const double
Constants.description: Like kPanSlop, but for more precise pointers like mice and trackpads.

Constants.title: kPrecisePointerScaleSlop → const double
Constants.description: Like kScaleSlop, but for more precise pointers like mice and trackpads.

Constants.title: kPressTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: The time that must elapse before a tap gesture sends onTapDown, if there's any doubt that the gesture is a tap.

Constants.title: kPrimaryButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to a cross-device behavior of "primary operation".

Constants.title: kPrimaryMouseButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to the primary mouse button.

Constants.title: kPrimaryStylusButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to the primary stylus button.

Constants.title: kScaleSlop → const double
Constants.description: The distance a touch has to travel for the framework to be confident that the gesture is a scale gesture.

Constants.title: kSecondaryButton → const int
Constants.description: The bit of PointerEvent.buttons that corresponds to a cross-device behavior of "secondary operation".

Constants.title: kSecondaryMouseButton → const int

Constants.title: kTouchSlop → const double

Constants.title: kWindowTouchSlop → const double

Constants.title: kZoomControlsTimeout → const Duration
Constants.description: The time for which zoom controls (e.g. in a map interface) are to be displayed on the screen, from the moment they were last requested.

Classes.title: MergeableMaterial
Classes.description: Displays a list of MergeableMaterialItem children. The list contains MaterialSlice items whose boundaries are either "merged" with adjacent items or separated by a MaterialGap. The children are distributed along the given mainAxis in the same way as the children of a ListBody. When the list of children changes, gaps are automatically animated open or closed as needed.

Classes.title: MergeableMaterialItem

Classes.title: MergeSemantics

Classes.title: MetaData
Classes.description: Holds opaque meta data in the render tree.

Classes.title: MinColumnWidth
Classes.description: Sizes the column such that it is the size that is the minimum of two column width specifications.

Classes.title: ModalBarrier
Classes.description: A widget that prevents the user from interacting with widgets behind itself.

Classes.title: ModalBottomSheetRoute<T>
Classes.description: A route that represents a Material Design modal bottom sheet.

Classes.title: ModalRoute<T>
Classes.description: A route that blocks interaction with previous routes.

Classes.title: MouseCursor
Classes.description: An interface for mouse cursor definitions.

Classes.title: MouseRegion

Since the dimensions of the rectangle are relative to those of the container, this class has no width and height members. To determine the width or height of the rectangle, convert it to a Rect using toRect() (passing the container's own Rect), and then examine that object.

Exceptions / Errors.title: FlutterError
Exceptions / Errors.description: Error class used to report Flutter-specific assertion failures and contract violations.

Exceptions / Errors.title: NetworkImageLoadException
Exceptions / Errors.description: The exception thrown when the HTTP request to load a network image fails.

Exceptions / Errors.title: TickerCanceled
Exceptions / Errors.description: Exception thrown by Ticker objects on the TickerFuture.orCancel future when the ticker is canceled.

Classes.title: Accumulator
Classes.description: Mutable wrapper of an integer that can be passed by reference to track a value across a recursive stack.

Classes.title: Alignment
Classes.description: A point within a rectangle.

Classes.title: AlignmentDirectional
Classes.description: An offset that's expressed as a fraction of a Size, but whose horizontal component is dependent on the writing direction.

Classes.title: AlignmentGeometry
Classes.description: Base class for Alignment that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

Classes.title: AssetBundleImageKey
Classes.description: Key for the image obtained by an AssetImage or ExactAssetImage.

Classes.title: AssetBundleImageProvider
Classes.description: A subclass of ImageProvider that knows about AssetBundles.

Classes.title: AssetImage
Classes.description: Fetches an image from an AssetBundle, having determined the exact image to use based on the context.

Classes.title: AutomaticNotchedShape
Classes.description: A NotchedShape created from ShapeBorders.

Classes.title: BeveledRectangleBorder
Classes.description: A rectangular border with flattened or "beveled" corners.

Classes.title: Border
Classes.description: A border of a box, comprised of four sides: top, right, bottom, left.

Classes.title: BorderDirectional
Classes.description: A border of a box, comprised of four sides, the lateral sides of which flip over based on the reading direction.

Classes.title: BorderRadius
Classes.description: An immutable set of radii for each corner of a rectangle.

Classes.title: BorderRadiusDirectional
Classes.description: An immutable set of radii for each corner of a rectangle, but with the corners specified in a manner dependent on the writing direction.

Classes.title: BorderRadiusGeometry
Classes.description: Base class for BorderRadius that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

Classes.title: BorderSide
Classes.description: A side of a border of a box.

Classes.title: BoxBorder
Classes.description: Base class for box borders that can paint as rectangles, circles, or rounded rectangles.

Classes.title: BoxDecoration
Classes.description: An immutable description of how to paint a box.

Classes.title: BoxPainter
Classes.description: A stateful class that can paint a particular Decoration.

Classes.title: BoxShadow
Classes.description: A shadow cast by a box.

Classes.title: Canvas
Classes.description: An interface for recording graphical operations.

Classes.title: CircleBorder
Classes.description: A border that fits a circle within the available space.

Classes.title: CircularNotchedRectangle
Classes.description: A rectangle with a smooth circular notch.

Classes.title: ClipContext
Classes.description: Clip utilities used by PaintingContext.

Classes.title: Color
Classes.description: An immutable color value in ARGB format.

Classes.title: ColorFilter
Classes.description: A description of a color filter to apply when drawing a shape or compositing a layer with a particular Paint. A color filter is a function that takes two colors, and outputs one color. When applied during compositing, it is independently applied to each pixel of the layer being drawn before the entire layer is merged with the destination.

Classes.title: ColorProperty
Classes.description: DiagnosticsProperty that has an Color as value.

Classes.title: ColorSwatch<T>
Classes.description: A color that has a small table of related colors called a "swatch".

Classes.title: ContinuousRectangleBorder
Classes.description: A rectangular border with smooth continuous transitions between the straight sides and the rounded corners.

Classes.title: Decoration
Classes.description: A description of a box decoration (a decoration applied to a Rect).

Classes.title: DecorationImage
Classes.description: An image for a box decoration.

Classes.title: DecorationImagePainter
Classes.description: The painter for a DecorationImage.

Classes.title: EdgeInsets
Classes.description: An immutable set of offsets in each of the four cardinal directions.

Classes.title: EdgeInsetsDirectional
Classes.description: An immutable set of offsets in each of the four cardinal directions, but whose horizontal components are dependent on the writing direction.

Classes.title: EdgeInsetsGeometry
Classes.description: Base class for EdgeInsets that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

Classes.title: ExactAssetImage
Classes.description: Fetches an image from an AssetBundle, associating it with the given scale.

Classes.title: FileImage
Classes.description: Decodes the given File object as an image, associating it with the given scale.

Classes.title: FittedSizes
Classes.description: The pair of sizes returned by applyBoxFit.

Classes.title: FlutterLogoDecoration
Classes.description: An immutable description of how to paint Flutter's logo.

Classes.title: FontFeature
Classes.description: A feature tag and value that affect the selection of glyphs in a font.

Classes.title: FontVariation
Classes.description: An axis tag and value that can be used to customize variable fonts.

Classes.title: FontWeight
Classes.description: The thickness of the glyphs used to draw the text.

Classes.title: FractionalOffset
Classes.description: An offset that's expressed as a fraction of a Size.

Classes.title: GlyphInfo
Classes.description: The measurements of a character (or a sequence of visually connected characters) within a paragraph.

Classes.title: Gradient
Classes.description: A 2D gradient.

Classes.title: GradientRotation
Classes.description: A GradientTransform that rotates the gradient around the center-point of its bounding box.

Classes.title: GradientTransform
Classes.description: Base class for transforming gradient shaders without applying the same transform to the entire canvas.

Classes.title: HSLColor
Classes.description: A color represented using alpha, hue, saturation, and lightness.

Classes.title: HSVColor
Classes.description: A color represented using alpha, hue, saturation, and value.

Classes.title: ImageCache
Classes.description: Class for caching images.

Classes.title: ImageCacheStatus
Classes.description: Information about how the ImageCache is tracking an image.

Classes.title: ImageChunkEvent
Classes.description: An immutable notification of image bytes that have been incrementally loaded.

Classes.title: ImageConfiguration
Classes.description: Configuration information passed to the ImageProvider.resolve method to select a specific image.

Classes.title: ImageInfo
Classes.description: A dart:ui.Image object with its corresponding scale.

Classes.title: ImageProvider<T extends Object>
Classes.description: Identifies an image without committing to the precise final asset. This allows a set of images to be identified and for the precise image to later be resolved based on the environment, e.g. the device pixel ratio.

Classes.title: ImageShader
Classes.description: A shader (as used by Paint.shader) that tiles an image.

Classes.title: ImageSizeInfo
Classes.description: Tracks the bytes used by a dart:ui.Image compared to the bytes needed to paint that image without scaling it.

Classes.title: ImageStream
Classes.description: A handle to an image resource.

Classes.title: ImageStreamCompleter
Classes.description: Base class for those that manage the loading of dart:ui.Image objects for ImageStreams.

Classes.title: ImageStreamCompleterHandle
Classes.description: An opaque handle that keeps an ImageStreamCompleter alive even if it has lost its last listener.

Classes.title: ImageStreamListener
Classes.description: Interface for receiving notifications about the loading of an image.

Classes.title: InlineSpan
Classes.description: An immutable span of inline content which forms part of a paragraph.

Classes.title: InlineSpanSemanticsInformation
Classes.description: The textual and semantic label information for an InlineSpan.

Classes.title: LinearBorder
Classes.description: An OutlinedBorder like BoxBorder that allows one to define a rectangular (box) border in terms of zero to four LinearBorderEdges, each of which is rendered as a single line.

Classes.title: LinearBorderEdge
Classes.description: Defines the relative size and alignment of one LinearBorder edge.

Classes.title: LinearGradient
Classes.description: A 2D linear gradient.

Classes.title: LineMetrics
Classes.description: LineMetrics stores the measurements and statistics of a single line in the paragraph.

Classes.title: Locale
Classes.description: An identifier used to select a user's language and formatting preferences.

Classes.title: MaskFilter
Classes.description: A mask filter to apply to shapes as they are painted. A mask filter is a function that takes a bitmap of color pixels, and returns another bitmap of color pixels.

Classes.title: MatrixUtils
Classes.description: Utility functions for working with matrices.

Classes.title: MemoryImage
Classes.description: Decodes the given Uint8List buffer as an image, associating it with the given scale.

Classes.title: MultiFrameImageStreamCompleter
Classes.description: Manages the decoding and scheduling of image frames.

Classes.title: NetworkImage
Classes.description: Fetches the given URL from the network, associating it with the given scale.

Classes.title: NotchedShape
Classes.description: A shape with a notch in its outline.

Classes.title: Offset
Classes.description: An immutable 2D floating-point offset.

Classes.title: OneFrameImageStreamCompleter
Classes.description: Manages the loading of dart:ui.Image objects for static ImageStreams (those with only one frame).

Classes.title: OutlinedBorder
Classes.description: A ShapeBorder that draws an outline with the width and color specified by side.

Classes.title: OvalBorder
Classes.description: A border that fits an elliptical shape.

Classes.title: Paint
Classes.description: A description of the style to use when drawing on a Canvas.

Classes.title: Path
Classes.description: A complex, one-dimensional subset of a plane.

Classes.title: PlaceholderDimensions
Classes.description: Holds the Size and baseline required to represent the dimensions of a placeholder in text.

Classes.title: PlaceholderSpan
Classes.description: An immutable placeholder that is embedded inline within text.

Classes.title: RadialGradient
Classes.description: A 2D radial gradient.

Classes.title: Radius

Properties.description: Whether to replace all shadows with solid color blocks. getter/setter pair

Properties.description: Adds debugging information to Timeline events related to RenderObject paints. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugImageOverheadAllowance ↔ int
Properties.description: The number of bytes an image must use before it triggers inversion when debugInvertOversizedImages is true. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugInvertOversizedImages ↔ bool
Properties.description: If true, the framework will color invert and horizontally flip images that have been decoded to a size taking at least debugImageOverheadAllowance bytes more than necessary. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugNetworkImageHttpClientProvider ↔ HttpClientProvider?
Properties.description: Provider from which NetworkImage will get its HttpClient in debug builds. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugOnPaintImage ↔ PaintImageCallback?

Properties.title: debugOnProfilePaint ↔ ProfilePaintCallback?
Properties.description: Callback invoked for every RenderObject painted each frame. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPaintBaselinesEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Causes each RenderBox to paint a line at each of its baselines. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPaintLayerBordersEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Causes each Layer to paint a box around its bounds. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPaintPointersEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Causes objects like RenderPointerListener to flash while they are being tapped. This can be useful to see how large the hit box is, e.g. when debugging buttons that are harder to hit than expected. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPaintSizeEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Causes each RenderBox to paint a box around its bounds, and some extra boxes, such as RenderPadding, to draw construction lines. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPaintTextLayoutBoxes ↔ bool
Properties.description: Causes each RenderParagraph to paint the layout boxes of its text. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPrint ↔ DebugPrintCallback
Properties.description: Prints a message to the console, which you can access using the "flutter" tool's "logs" command ("flutter logs"). getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPrintLayouts ↔ bool
Properties.description: Log the dirty render objects that are laid out each frame. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPrintMarkNeedsLayoutStacks ↔ bool
Properties.description: Log the call stacks that mark render objects as needing layout. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugPrintMarkNeedsPaintStacks ↔ bool
Properties.description: Log the call stacks that mark render objects as needing paint. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugProfileLayoutsEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Adds Timeline events for every RenderObject layout. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugProfilePaintsEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Adds Timeline events for every RenderObject painted. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugRepaintRainbowEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Overlay a rotating set of colors when repainting layers in debug mode. getter/setter pair

Properties.title: debugRepaintTextRainbowEnabled ↔ bool
Properties.description: Overlay a rotating set of colors when repainting text in debug mode. getter/setter pair

Classes.description: Class that programmatically interacts with widgets and the test environment.

Enums.title: EnginePhase
Enums.description: Phases that can be reached by WidgetTester.pumpWidget and TestWidgetsFlutterBinding.pump.

Enums.title: LiveTestWidgetsFlutterBindingFramePolicy
Enums.description: Available policies for how a LiveTestWidgetsFlutterBinding should paint frames.

Enums.title: Plurality
Enums.description: Describes how a string of text should be pluralized.

Enums.title: TestBindingEventSource
Enums.description: Parts of the system that can generate pointer events that reach the test binding.

Enums.title: TextInputAction
Enums.description: An action the user has requested the text input control to perform.

Mixins.title: ChainedFinderMixin<CandidateType>
Mixins.description: A mixin that applies additional filtering to the results of a parent Finder.

Mixins.title: LocalComparisonOutput
Mixins.description: A mixin for use in golden file comparators that run locally and provide output.

Mixins.title: MatchFinderMixin<CandidateType>
Mixins.description: A mixin for creating finders that search candidates for those that match a given pattern.

Mixins.title: TestDefaultBinaryMessengerBinding
Mixins.description: Overrides the ServicesBinding's binary messenger logic to use TestDefaultBinaryMessenger.

Extensions.title: TestBasicMessageChannelExtension on BasicMessageChannel<T>
Extensions.description: Shim to support the obsolete setMockMessageHandler and checkMockMessageHandler methods on BasicMessageChannel in tests.

Extensions.title: TestBinaryMessengerExtension on BinaryMessenger
Extensions.description: Shim to support the obsolete setMockMessageHandler and checkMockMessageHandler methods on BinaryMessenger in tests.

Extensions.title: TestMethodChannelExtension on MethodChannel
Extensions.description: Shim to support the obsolete setMockMethodCallHandler and checkMockMethodCallHandler methods on MethodChannel in tests.

Properties.description: Matches a Future that completes successfully with any value. final

Properties.title: doesNotComplete → Matcher
Properties.description: Matches a Future that does not complete. final

Properties.description: Returns a StreamMatcher that asserts that the stream emits a "done" event. final

Properties.title: isLinux → bool
Properties.description: Whether the test is running on the Linux operating system. no setter

Properties.description: Whether the test is running on the Windows operating system. no setter

Functions.description: Provides an iterable that efficiently returns all the SemanticsNodes rooted at the given SemanticsNode. See CachingIterable for details.

Functions.description: Matches a Future that completes successfully with a value that matches matcher.

Functions.title: contains(Object? expected) → Matcher
Functions.description: Returns a matcher that matches if the match argument contains the expected value.

Functions.title: containsAll(Iterable expected) → Matcher

Functions.title: containsOnce(Object? expected) → Matcher
Functions.description: Matches Iterables where exactly one element matches the expected value, and all other elements don't match.

Functions.title: containsPair(Object? key, Object? valueOrMatcher) → Matcher
Functions.description: Returns a matcher which matches maps containing the key-value pair with key => valueOrMatcher.

Classes.title: DateSymbols
Classes.description: This holds onto information about how a particular locale formats dates. It contains mostly strings, e.g. what the names of months or weekdays are, but also indicates things like the first day of the week. We expect the data for instances of these to be generated out of ICU or a similar reference source. This is used in conjunction with the date_time_patterns, which defines for a particular locale the different named formats that will make use of this data.

Properties.title: en_USSymbols → DateSymbols
Properties.description: We hard-code the locale data for en_US here so that there's at least one locale always available. final

Functions.title: dateTimePatternMap() → Map<String, Map<String, String>>
Functions.description: Returns a Map from locale names to another Map that goes from skeletons to the locale-specific formatting patterns. Internal use only. Call initializeDateFormatting instead.

Classes.title: Bidi

Classes.title: BidiFormatter
Classes.description: Bidi stands for Bi-directional text. According to Wikipedia: Bi-directional text is text containing text in both text directionalities, both right-to-left (RTL) and left-to-right (LTR). It generally involves text containing different types of alphabets, but may also refer to boustrophedon, which is changing text directionality in each row.

Constants.description: An annotation for methods to request that dart2js always inline the method.

Classes.title: Immutable
Classes.description: Used to annotate a class.

Classes.title: RecordUse
Classes.description: Annotates a static method to be recorded.

Classes.title: Required
Classes.description: Used to annotate a named parameter p in a method or function f.

Classes.title: UseResult
Classes.description: See useResult for more details.

Constants.title: alwaysThrows → const _AlwaysThrows
Constants.description: Used to annotate a function f. Indicates that f always throws an exception. Any functions that override f, in class inheritance, are also expected to conform to this contract.

Constants.title: awaitNotRequired → const _AwaitNotRequired
Constants.description: Used to annotate a Future-returning function (including constructors, getters, methods, and operators), or a Future-typed field (including top-level, instance, and static) f. Indicates that the Future value that f returns does not need to be awaited. Any methods that override f in class inheritance, are also expected to conform to this contract.

Constants.title: checked → const _Checked
Constants.description: Used to annotate a parameter of an instance method that overrides another method.

Constants.title: doNotStore → const _DoNotStore
Constants.description: Used to annotate a method, getter, top-level function, or top-level getter to indicate that the value obtained by invoking it should not be stored in a field or top-level variable. The annotation can also be applied to a class to implicitly annotate all of the valid members of the class, or applied to a library to annotate all of the valid members of the library, including classes. If a value returned by an element marked as doNotStore is returned from a function or getter, that function or getter should be similarly annotated.

Constants.title: doNotSubmit → const _DoNotSubmit
Constants.description: Used to annotate an optional parameter, method, getter or top-level getter or function that is not intended to be accessed in checked-in code, but might be ephemerally used during development or local testing.

Constants.title: experimental → const _Experimental
Constants.description: Used to annotate a library, or any declaration that is part of the public interface of a library (such as top-level members, class members, and function parameters) to indicate that the annotated API is experimental and may be removed or changed at any-time without updating the version of the containing package, despite the fact that it would otherwise be a breaking change.

Constants.title: factory → const _Factory
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance or static method m. Indicates that m must either be abstract or must return a newly allocated object or null. In addition, every method that either implements or overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: immutable → const Immutable
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class C. Indicates that C and all subtypes of C must be immutable.

Constants.title: internal → const _Internal
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration which should only be used from within the package in which it is declared, and which should not be exposed from said package's public API.

Constants.title: isTest → const _IsTest
Constants.description: Used to annotate a test framework function that runs a single test.

Constants.title: isTestGroup → const _IsTestGroup
Constants.description: Used to annotate a test framework function that runs a group of tests.

Constants.title: literal → const _Literal
Constants.description: Used to annotate a const constructor c. Indicates that any invocation of the constructor must use the keyword const unless one or more of the arguments to the constructor is not a compile-time constant.

Constants.title: mustBeConst → const _MustBeConst
Constants.description: Used to annotate a parameter which should be constant.

Constants.title: mustBeOverridden → const _MustBeOverridden
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member m declared on a class or mixin C. Indicates that every concrete subclass of C must directly override m.

Constants.title: mustCallSuper → const _MustCallSuper
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m. Indicates that every invocation of a member that overrides m must also invoke m. In addition, every method that overrides m is implicitly annotated with this same annotation.

Constants.title: nonVirtual → const _NonVirtual
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member (method, getter, setter, operator, or field) m in a class C or mixin M. Indicates that m should not be overridden in any classes that extend or mixin C or M.

Constants.title: optionalTypeArgs → const _OptionalTypeArgs
Constants.description: Used to annotate a class, mixin, extension, function, method, or typedef declaration C. Indicates that any type arguments declared on C are to be treated as optional.

Constants.title: protected → const _Protected
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member in a class or mixin which is meant to be visible only within the declaring library, and to other instance members of the class or mixin, and their subtypes.

Constants.title: redeclare → const _Redeclare
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member of an extension type that redeclares a member from a superinterface.

Constants.title: reopen → const _Reopen
Constants.description: Annotation for intentionally loosening restrictions on subtyping that would otherwise cause lint warnings to be produced by the implicit_reopen lint.

Constants.title: required → const Required
Constants.description: Used to annotate a named parameter p in a method or function f. Indicates that every invocation of f must include an argument corresponding to p, despite the fact that p would otherwise be an optional parameter.

Constants.title: sealed → const _Sealed
Constants.description: Annotation marking a class as not allowed as a super-type outside of the current package.

Constants.title: useResult → const UseResult
Constants.description: Used to annotate a method, field, or getter within a class, mixin, or extension, or a or top-level getter, variable or function to indicate that the value obtained by invoking it should be used. A value is considered used if it is assigned to a variable, passed to a function, or used as the target of an invocation, or invoked (if the result is itself a function).

Constants.title: virtual → const _Virtual
Constants.description: Used to annotate a field that is allowed to be overridden in Strong Mode.

Constants.title: visibleForOverriding → const _VisibleForOverriding
Constants.description: Used to annotate an instance member that was made public so that it could be overridden but that is not intended to be referenced from outside the defining library.

Constants.title: visibleForTesting → const _VisibleForTesting
Constants.description: Used to annotate a declaration that was made public, so that it is more visible than otherwise necessary, to make code testable.

Functions.description: Constructs an OpenGL infinite projection matrix in infiniteMatrix. fovYRadians specifies the field of view angle, in radians, in the y direction. aspectRatio specifies the aspect ratio that determines the field of view in the x direction. Model matrix is commonly used to compute a object location/orientation into the full model-view stack.

Functions.title: generateNormals(Vector3List normals, Vector3List positions, Uint16List indices) → void
Functions.description: Generate vertex normals based on a list of positions and indices. normals is assumed to be zeroed out, and much match positions in length. indices is assumed to represent a triangle list.

Functions.title: generateTangents(Vector4List tangents, Vector3List positions, Vector3List normals, Vector2List texCoords, Uint16List indices) → void
Functions.description: Generate vertex tangents based on a list of positions, normals, texCoords and indices. tangents is assumed to be zeroed out, and much match positions, normals, and texCoords in length. indices is assumed to represent a triangle list. Tangents are returned as Vector4s. The X, Y, and Z component represent the tangent and the W component represents the direction of the bitangent which can be generated as: vec4 bitangent = cross(normal, tangent.xyz) * tangent.w; Derived from the granddaddy of all tangent generation functions: http://www.terathon.com/code/tangent.html

Exceptions / Errors.title: ElementClickInterceptedException
Exceptions / Errors.description: The Element Click command could not be completed because the element receiving the events is obscuring the element that was requested clicked.

Exceptions / Errors.title: ElementNotInteractableException
Exceptions / Errors.description: A command could not be completed because the element is not pointer- or keyboard interactable.

Exceptions / Errors.title: InsecureCertificateException
Exceptions / Errors.description: Navigation caused the user agent to hit a certificate warning, which is usually the result of an expired or invalid TLS certificate.

Exceptions / Errors.title: InvalidSessionIdException
Exceptions / Errors.description: Occurs if the given session id is not in the list of active sessions, meaning the session either does not exist or that it’s not active.

Exceptions / Errors.title: NoSuchCookieException
Exceptions / Errors.description: No cookie matching the given path name was found amongst the associated cookies of the current browsing context’s active document.

Exceptions / Errors.title: UnableToCaptureScreenException
Exceptions / Errors.description: A screen capture was made impossible.

Exceptions / Errors.title: UnknownMethodException
Exceptions / Errors.description: The requested command matched a known URL but did not match an method for that URL.

Exceptions / Errors.title: UnsupportedOperationException
Exceptions / Errors.description: Indicates that a command that should have executed properly cannot be supported for some reason.

Exceptions / Errors.title: WebDriverException
Exceptions / Errors.description: Base exception for anything unexpected happened in Web Driver requests.
